pattern: An in-order walk of a BST is sorted; exactly two such readings are out of order when two nodes are swapped, so find that pair and swap their values back.
hints: In-order traversal of a correct BST gives strictly increasing values; a single swap breaks that ordering in one or two places.
Scan the in-order sequence for descents (a value bigger than the next): the first descent's larger node and the last descent's smaller node are the two culprits.
If the two swapped nodes are adjacent in the in-order sequence there is one descent; otherwise two. Swap the two offending node values, not their positions.

edge_inputs.description: two adjacent in-order nodes swapped (single descent)
minimal tree of two nodes, root and child swapped
root and a deep node swapped

stress.description: build a valid BST from distinct values, then swap two random nodes' values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 200))
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(-100000, 100001), n))
    # balanced BST from the sorted values -> level-order array with nulls
    nodes = {}
    def build(lo, hi):
        if lo > hi:
            return None
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        idx = build.counter
        build.counter += 1
        node = {'val': vals[mid], 'l': None, 'r': None}
        node['l'] = build(lo, mid - 1)
        node['r'] = build(mid + 1, hi)
        return node
    build.counter = 0
    root = build(0, n - 1)
    # collect nodes (BFS) then swap two random ones' values
    order = []
    q = [root]
    while q:
        nd = q.pop(0)
        if nd is None:
            continue
        order.append(nd)
        q.append(nd['l']); q.append(nd['r'])
    i, j = rng.sample(range(len(order)), 2)
    order[i]['val'], order[j]['val'] = order[j]['val'], order[i]['val']
    # serialize to level-order array with trailing-null trim
    arr = []
    q = [root]
    while q:
        nd = q.pop(0)
        if nd is None:
            arr.append(None); continue
        arr.append(nd['val'])
        q.append(nd['l']); q.append(nd['r'])
    while arr and arr[-1] is None:
        arr.pop()
    return [arr]
stress.note: exactly two values are swapped so the unique fix is well defined
